field.label: Engine API URL
field.comment: Base URL of your self-hosted OpenSalesTax engine, e.g. https://ost.example.com

field.label: API Token (optional)
field.comment: Bearer token if your engine instance requires authentication. Stored encrypted.

field.label: Fail Hard on Engine Error
field.comment: If Yes, an unreachable engine blocks checkout. If No (default), the module falls back to Magento's built-in tax tables and logs a warning.

field.label: Restrict and Pin Engine URL
field.comment: If Yes, two defenses activate. At save time, the Engine API URL must resolve to a public IP â€” RFC 1918 / loopback / link-local / reserved ranges are rejected. The resolved IP is then pinned: subsequent engine calls dial that IP via cURL CURLOPT_RESOLVE regardless of what DNS returns, defending against DNS rebinding. If your engine's IP rotates, you must re-save the URL to refresh the pin. If No (default), localhost / RFC1918 / link-local hosts are permitted â€” the supported pattern when Magento and OST run on the same VM.

group.label: Category Mapping
group.comment: Map each Magento product tax class to an OpenSalesTax category so the engine applies the correct per-state rules (clothing exemptions, grocery rates, prescription-drug exemptions, etc.). Stored as a JSON object in core_config_data. Tax classes not listed fall back to general. Set a value to empty string ("") to mark a tax class non-taxable. An inline admin editor with dropdowns lands in a v1.3.1 follow-up.

field.label: Tax Class to OST Category Mapping (JSON)
field.comment: JSON object mapping Magento tax class id to OST category. Valid OST categories: general, clothing, groceries, prescription_drugs, prepared_food, digital_goods, or "" (skip line / non-taxable). Example: {"2":"clothing","3":"groceries","5":"digital_goods","6":""}.
